text: Profits are higher than last year.
Sales went up by ten percent.
David, here are the numbers for last month. Sales increased a lot.
That is great news, Maisa. How much did they go up?
They went up by ten percent. Our profits grew and are higher than last year.
Excellent. And what about our costs?
Good question. Costs decreased in March. They are lower now.
So sales rose and costs fell. That is a very good result.
Yes. But one number dropped fast. Can we look at the trend together?
Of course. Let me check it with you now.
Sales increased by ten percent last month.
Our costs are lower than before.

Hi Maisa, this is David. Let me tell you about our numbers last month. Sales increased a lot. They went up by twelve percent. Our profits grew, and they are higher than last year. But not everything went up. Our costs increased too, and one client dropped their order. That number fell fast. The good news is the general trend is positive. Sales are higher, and the team is happy. Please check the report and tell me what you think. Thank you!

Hello, this is Sarah from the finance team. I want to tell you about my results. Last month was not easy. Our sales decreased a little. They went down by five percent. Our costs are higher now, so profits are lower than last year. But there is good news too. One product grew fast. Sales for that product increased every week. The trend for the new product is very good. If a number falls, we look at it together. Have a great day!
Good morning, David. Here are the numbers for last month. Sales increased by ten percent. Profits are higher than last year. Costs decreased in March. Thank you very much.
Sales went up by ten percent.
Profits are higher than last year.
Sales increased a lot in March.
Sales increased by ten percent last month.
Our costs are lower than before.
